name: Alcantara Space Center

overview.value: The Alcântara Space Center (Centro Espacial de Alcântara, CEA), formerly known as Alcântara Launch Center, is a space center and launching facility of the Brazilian Space Agency in the city of Alcântara, on Brazil's northern Atlantic coast in the state of Maranhão. It is operated by the Brazilian Air Force (Comando da Aeronáutica). Construction began in 1982 and the first launch, the sounding rocket Sonda 2 XV-53, occurred on 21 February 1990. The site is the closest launch base to the equator, an advantage shared with the Guiana Space Centre for launching geosynchronous satellites.

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add.
